chapters.title: The Subjunctive — Congiuntivo
chapters.description: Master the congiuntivo to express doubt, emotion, and opinion.

lessons.explanation: Verbs of opinion/doubt trigger congiuntivo: pensare, credere, sperare, dubitare. Certainty uses indicativo.

lessons.prompt: Voglio che tu ___ la verità. (I want you to tell the truth.)
lessons.explanation: 'Volere che' triggers congiuntivo. Congiuntivo of 'dire' for tu = dica.

lessons.prompt: Translate: 'Although he is tired, he keeps working'
lessons.correctAnswer: Sebbene sia stanco continua a lavorare
lessons.hint: 'Sebbene' (although) always takes congiuntivo. 'Sia' = congiuntivo of essere.

lessons.prompt: What is the congiuntivo presente of 'avere' for 'che lui'?
lessons.explanation: Congiuntivo of avere: che io/tu/lui/lei abbia, noi abbiamo, voi abbiate, loro abbiano.

lessons.prompt: Benché ___ ricco, vive semplicemente. (Although he is rich, he lives simply.)
lessons.explanation: 'Benché' (although) → congiuntivo. 'Sia' = congiuntivo of essere (3rd person singular).

chapters.title: Italian Idioms
chapters.description: Sound like a native Italian with common idiomatic expressions.

lessons.prompt: What does 'In bocca al lupo' mean?
lessons.explanation: Literally 'into the wolf's mouth' — the standard way to wish good luck. Reply: 'Crepi!' (May it die!).

lessons.prompt: Non mi rompere le ___! (Don't break my chops / Stop annoying me!)
lessons.explanation: Literally 'don't break my boxes' — a polite version of a common vulgar phrase meaning 'stop annoying me'.

lessons.prompt: Translate the Italian idiom for 'to cost an arm and a leg'
lessons.hint: Literally 'to cost an eye from the head'.

lessons.prompt: What does 'avere le mani in pasta' mean?
lessons.explanation: Literally 'to have your hands in the dough' — to be deeply involved in a matter (often business or politics).

lessons.prompt: Ogni ___ a modo suo. (To each their own.)
lessons.explanation: 'Ogni testa a modo suo' = each head in its own way. Equivalent of 'to each their own'.

chapters.title: Condizionale & Hypotheticals
chapters.description: Express conditions, wishes, and polite requests using the conditional.

lessons.prompt: Which sentence is a real condition?
options: Se fossi ricco, comprerei una villa
lessons.explanation: Real condition: se + presente → presente/imperativo. Unreal: se + congiuntivo imperfetto → condizionale.

lessons.prompt: Se studiassi di più, ___ agli esami. (If you studied more, you would pass the exams.)
lessons.explanation: Unreal present condition: se + congiuntivo imperfetto → condizionale presente.

lessons.prompt: Translate: 'Could you pass me the salt please?' (polite)
lessons.correctAnswer: Potrebbe passarmi il sale per favore
lessons.hint: 'Potrebbe' = condizionale of potere (3rd person). More polite than 'può'.

lessons.prompt: What is the condizionale presente of 'volere' for 'io'?
lessons.explanation: 'Vorrei' = I would like. One of the most useful Italian phrases. Irregular conditional stem: vorr-.

lessons.prompt: Se avessi saputo, non ___ venuto. (If I had known, I would not have come.)
lessons.explanation: Past hypothetical: se + congiuntivo trapassato → condizionale passato. 'Non sarei venuto' = would not have come.
